title: The Case of the Missing Water Hole
synopsis: When all the animals' favorite water hole mysteriously disappears overnight, young detective Nia teams up with her animal friends to solve the puzzle and save the day in the African savanna.

pages.text: Nia loved living near the big savanna. Every morning, she would watch the animals come to drink at the water hole. The elephants would splash with their trunks. The zebras would drink in long rows. The giraffes would bend their long necks down, down, down to reach the water.

illustration.description: A young girl with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing on a small hill overlooking a busy water hole in the savanna, with elephants, zebras, and giraffes drinking water, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, textured painterly style with golden grass and orange sunset light
illustration.alt: Nia watching animals at the water hole

pages.text: But this morning was different. Very different! Nia ran to her favorite watching spot and gasped. The water hole was gone! Where the big blue water used to be, there was only dry, cracked mud. 'Oh no!' said Nia. 'Where did all the water go?'

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes pointing at an empty, dried-up water hole with cracked mud, looking surprised and worried, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors showing the contrast between golden grass and the brown dried earth
illustration.alt: Nia discovering the empty water hole

pages.text: Soon, all the animals gathered around the empty water hole. They looked very sad and very thirsty. Kesi the elephant trumpeted sadly. Jengo the zebra pawed at the dry ground. Twiga the giraffe stretched her neck high to look for water somewhere else. But there was no water anywhere!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes surrounded by sad animals - an elephant, zebra, and giraffe - all looking at the empty water hole, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals with drooping ears and heads, textured painterly style
illustration.alt: Sad animals gathering around the empty water hole

pages.text: Nia put her hands on her hips like a real detective. 'Don't worry, friends,' she said to all the animals. 'I will solve this mystery! Water doesn't just disappear. There must be clues somewhere.' Kesi the elephant trumpeted with hope. Maybe Nia could help!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing with hands on hips in a detective pose, with hopeful animals looking at her including an elephant raising its trunk, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, bold confident strokes showing determination
illustration.alt: Nia taking on the detective role

pages.text: Nia walked slowly around the empty water hole, looking for clues. She looked at the ground. She looked at the trees. She looked at the rocks. Then she saw something interesting! There were strange marks in the mud. They looked like... tire tracks!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes crouched down examining tire tracks in the dried mud near the water hole, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, textured painterly style emphasizing the tracks in the earth
illustration.alt: Nia examining tire tracks in the mud

pages.text: 'Tire tracks!' said Nia. 'Someone came here with a big truck. But why?' She followed the tracks with her eyes. They led away from the water hole, through the tall golden grass, and toward the hills. 'Come on, friends,' Nia called to the animals. 'Let's follow these tracks!'

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes pointing toward tire tracks leading through golden grass toward distant hills, with curious animals behind her, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, golden grass swaying in the breeze
illustration.alt: Nia and animals following the tire tracks

pages.text: Nia and her animal friends followed the tire tracks through the savanna. Twiga the giraffe used her tall height to look ahead. Jengo the zebra used his fast legs to scout around. Kesi the elephant used her good nose to smell for clues. They made a great detective team!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes walking with a tall giraffe looking ahead, a zebra running nearby, and an elephant with trunk raised sniffing, all following tire tracks through golden grass, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals working together

pages.text: The tracks led them up a small hill. At the top, Nia heard a strange sound. 'Listen!' she whispered. It sounded like... water! Splashing, flowing water! But where was it coming from? Twiga pointed her long neck toward a big truck parked behind some trees.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes with one finger to her lips in a 'quiet' gesture, standing with her animal friends on a hill looking toward a truck partially hidden behind acacia trees, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors with orange sunset light
illustration.alt: Nia and friends discovering the truck

pages.text: They crept closer to the truck. And there they saw the most wonderful sight! The truck had a big tank full of water. Their water! A man was using the water to fill small containers. But why had he taken all the water from their water hole?

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes peeking from behind a tree with her animal friends, watching a man with a water truck filling containers, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, textured painterly style showing the mystery being revealed
illustration.alt: Discovering the water truck and man

pages.text: Nia took a deep breath and walked up to the man. 'Excuse me,' she said politely. 'Why did you take all the water from our water hole? The animals are very thirsty.' The man looked surprised to see Nia and all her animal friends. He looked a little embarrassed too.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing confidently in front of a surprised-looking man in work clothes, with elephant, giraffe, and zebra behind her for support, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, bold confident strokes
illustration.alt: Nia confronting the man about the missing water

pages.text: 'Oh my!' said the man. 'I didn't know the animals needed this water! I work for the village nearby. Our well broke, and we needed water for the people. I thought this water hole was empty sometimes. I'm so sorry! I didn't mean to hurt the animals.'

illustration.description: The man gesturing apologetically toward Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, with concerned animals listening, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals and humans showing understanding and communication
illustration.alt: The man explaining his actions apologetically

pages.text: Nia smiled with her warm, kind eyes. 'I understand,' she said. 'People need water too. But so do the animals. Can we find a way to help everyone?' The man's face lit up. 'I have an idea!' he said. 'What if we share the water? I'll only take half, and leave half for the animals.'

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes shaking hands with the smiling man while happy animals watch, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, golden grass, showing cooperation and friendship being formed
illustration.alt: Nia and the man agreeing to share the water

pages.text: And that's exactly what they did! The man pumped half the water back into the water hole. Soon, Kesi, Jengo, Twiga, and all their friends were drinking cool, fresh water again. 'Thank you, Detective Nia!' trumpeted Kesi. 'You solved the mystery and saved the day!' Nia felt proud. She had helped both the people and the animals. Everyone was happy, and everyone had water. That was the best solution of all!

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes standing proudly beside the restored water hole with happy animals drinking - elephants splashing, zebras in rows, giraffes bending down - while the man waves goodbye with his truck in the background, painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, orange sunset, textured painterly style showing celebration and harmony
illustration.alt: Happy ending with animals drinking at the restored water hole

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What did Nia find in the dried mud around the water hole?
questions.explanation: Nia discovered tire tracks in the mud, which were the first important clue in solving the mystery of the missing water.

questions.question: Why did the man take the water from the water hole?
choices: his village well broke and people needed water
questions.explanation: The man explained that his village's well had broken and the people needed water, so he took it from the water hole not knowing the animals depended on it.

questions.question: How did Nia and the man solve the problem?
choices: they found a new water hole
they decided to share the water
questions.explanation: Nia and the man agreed to share the water - he would take only half for the village and leave half for the animals.

questions.question: Which animal helped by looking ahead with her tall height?
questions.explanation: Twiga the giraffe used her tall height to look ahead and help the detective team follow the clues.

questions.question: What made Nia a good detective?
choices: she looked for clues and wanted to help everyone
questions.explanation: Nia was a good detective because she carefully looked for clues, followed them, and wanted to find a solution that would help both the animals and the people.
